summary: Matched five simple Yoshi guard-state callback wrappers in ftYs_Guard.c by replacing nonmatching asm placeholders with direct calls into shared common guard helper functions. The PR covered Yoshi GuardDamage physical/collision callbacks and GuardOn_1 IASA/physical/collision callbacks. Review discussion was minimal but positive, with a maintainer thanking the first-time contributor and pointing them to the Discord for questions.
what_changed: Implemented ftYs_GuardDamage_Phys as a direct wrapper around ftCo_GuardSetOff_Phys.
Implemented ftYs_GuardDamage_Coll as a direct wrapper around ftCo_GuardSetOff_Coll.
Implemented ftYs_GuardOn_1_IASA as a direct wrapper around ftCo_GuardReflect_IASA.
Implemented ftYs_GuardOn_1_Phys as a direct wrapper around ftCo_GuardReflect_Phys.
Implemented ftYs_GuardOn_1_Coll as a direct wrapper around ftCo_GuardReflect_Coll.
Removed five nonmatching placeholder comments of the form /// #function_name in src/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_Guard.c.
smart_moves: Used existing common fighter guard helper functions rather than duplicating guard logic in Yoshi-specific code.
Recognized these Yoshi callbacks were thin state-function forwarding wrappers, allowing clean one-call C implementations.
Kept the implementations minimal, which is often the correct matching tactic for character-specific callback slots that delegate to shared common behavior.
Used Fighter_GObj* for the implemented wrappers, matching the called common helper APIs shown in the diff.
decomp_lessons: When a character-specific guard callback has no apparent special logic, compare it against common ftCo guard helpers; it may be a one-line wrapper.
GuardDamage Phys/Coll callbacks can map to GuardSetOff common callbacks for physical and collision handling.
GuardOn reflect-style callbacks can map to GuardReflect common IASA, Phys, and Coll helpers.
Small matching PRs can be valuable when they replace asm placeholders with clear shared-helper calls and reduce the unmatched surface area.
For simple forwarding functions, preserving the exact callback signature type expected by the helper can be the entire matching trick.
naming_conventions: Yoshi-specific functions in this file use the ftYs_ prefix.
Common fighter guard helpers use the ftCo_ prefix.
State callback suffixes follow the standard Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll naming pattern.
The matched Yoshi functions mirror common helper categories: GuardDamage_Phys to GuardSetOff_Phys, GuardDamage_Coll to GuardSetOff_Coll, GuardOn_1_IASA/Phys/Coll to GuardReflect_IASA/Phys/Coll.
Unmatched functions were marked with /// #function_name comments before being replaced by C definitions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace an asm/nonmatching placeholder with a direct call wrapper when the function body is only delegation to an existing helper.
Search adjacent fighter or common guard functions for matching callback behavior before attempting to reconstruct logic manually.
Use the shared ftCo guard helper names as matching anchors for character-specific guard states.
Minimal wrapper functions may match without local variables or additional control flow when the original assembly is just a branch/call to a common routine.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Nice job, thanks! Feel free to ask anything in the Discord channel linked in the README.

follow_up_queries: Find other character-specific GuardDamage_Phys or GuardDamage_Coll placeholders that may delegate to ftCo_GuardSetOff_Phys or ftCo_GuardSetOff_Coll.
Find other GuardOn reflect-state IASA/Phys/Coll placeholders that may delegate to ftCo_GuardReflect_IASA, ftCo_GuardReflect_Phys, or ftCo_GuardReflect_Coll.
Audit ftYs_Guard.c for remaining /// # placeholders near Yoshi shield and guard states.
Compare adjacent fighter guard files for the same callback-wrapper pattern.
